notes.anchor: On the sixth day before the Ides of May
notes.note: The Ides of May fell on the 15th; reckoned inclusively in the Roman manner, the sixth day before is 10 May 51 BC.

The tribuni plebis were ten annually elected magistrates who could veto official acts and convene the plebs; Cicero counts on Furnius, a tribune-designate for 50 BC, to block any extension of his Cilician command.
Prorogation: the Senate could prolong a governor's term beyond a year. Cicero, dreading a longer absence, lobbies friends to prevent his proconsulship of Cilicia from being extended.

notes.anchor: a rumour about the Transpadanes that they had been instructed to elect four-men commissioners
notes.note: The Transpadanes (communities of Cisalpine Gaul north of the Po) held only Latin rights; Caesar favoured giving them full citizenship, which would entail their towns electing the quattuorviri (boards of four) typical of Roman municipia. The rumour touched a flashpoint between Caesar and the Senate.
